audio(3) Library Functions Manual audio(3)

NAME

audio - Audio

SYNOPSIS

Files


file analysis.c
Audio Analysis Implementation. file audio.c
🔊 Audio capture and playback using PortAudio with buffer management file mixer.c
🎚️ Real-time audio mixer with ducking, gain control, and multi-stream blending file opus.c
Opus audio codec implementation. file analysis.h
Audio Analysis and Debugging Interface. file audio.h
🔊 Audio Capture and Playback Interface for ascii-chat file client_pipeline.h
Unified client-side audio processing pipeline. file mixer.h
Multi-Source Audio Mixing and Processing System. file opus.h
Opus audio codec wrapper for real-time encoding/decoding.

Data Structures

Audio context for full-duplex capture and playback. struct audio_device_info_t
Audio device information structure. struct audio_batch_info_t
Parsed audio batch packet header information. struct compressor_t

Noise gate settings and state. struct highpass_filter_t
High-pass filter settings and state. struct lowpass_filter_t
Low-pass filter state. struct ducking_t
Ducking system settings and state. struct mixer_t
Main mixer structure for multi-source audio processing. struct opus_codec_t

Macros


#define AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE 48000
Audio sample rate (48kHz professional quality, Opus-compatible). #define AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER 480
Audio frames per buffer (480 = 10ms at 48kHz, matches WebRTC AEC3 frame size). #define AUDIO_CHANNELS 1
Number of audio channels (1 = mono). #define AUDIO_BUFFER_SIZE (AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER * AUDIO_CHANNELS)
Total audio buffer size (frames × channels).

Ducking attack time in nanoseconds. uint64_t ducking_t::release_ns
Ducking release time in nanoseconds. float ducking_t::attack_coeff
Attack coefficient (converted from attack_ms). float ducking_t::release_coeff
Release coefficient (converted from release_ms). float * ducking_t::envelope
Per-source envelope follower state (linear, allocated per source). float * ducking_t::gain
Per-source ducking gain (linear, calculated from envelope). int mixer_t::num_sources

Sample rate in Hz (e.g., 44100). audio_ring_buffer_t ** mixer_t::source_buffers
Array of pointers to client audio ring buffers. const char ** mixer_t::source_ids
Array of client ID strings (one per source slot). bool * mixer_t::source_active
Array of active flags (true if source is active). uint64_t mixer_t::active_sources_mask
Bitset of active sources (bit i = source i is active, O(1) iteration). uint8_t mixer_t::source_id_to_index [256]
Hash table mapping client_id → mixer source index (uses hash function for strings). const char * mixer_t::source_id_at_hash [256]
Client ID strings stored at each hash index for collision detection. rwlock_t mixer_t::source_lock

Crowd scaling exponent (typically 0.5 for sqrt scaling). float mixer_t::base_gain
Base gain before crowd scaling is applied. ducking_t mixer_t::ducking
Ducking system (active speaker detection and attenuation). compressor_t mixer_t::compressor

Audio Mixing Configuration


#define MIXER_MAX_SOURCES 32
Maximum number of simultaneous audio sources. #define MIXER_FRAME_SIZE 256
Number of samples processed per audio frame.

Detailed Description

This header provides audio capture and playback functionality using PortAudio, enabling real-time audio streaming for ascii-chat video chat sessions.

CORE FEATURES:

  • Real-time audio capture from microphone/input devices
  • Real-time audio playback to speakers/output devices
  • Thread-safe ring buffers for audio data
  • Low-latency audio processing
  • Platform-specific real-time priority scheduling
  • Configurable audio parameters (sample rate, buffer size)

AUDIO ARCHITECTURE:

The audio system uses PortAudio for cross-platform audio I/O:

  • Single full-duplex stream for simultaneous capture and playback
  • Single callback receives both render and capture at EXACT same instant
  • Perfect timing for WebRTC AEC3 echo cancellation (no ring buffer delay)
  • Ring buffers only for network I/O (playback from jitter buffer, capture to encoder)
  • Automatic device enumeration and selection

AUDIO PARAMETERS:

  • Sample Rate: 48kHz (professional quality, Opus-compatible)
  • Channels: Mono (1 channel)
  • Buffer Size: 256 frames per buffer (low latency)
  • Format: 32-bit floating point samples

THREAD SAFETY:

  • Audio context state protected by mutex
  • Ring buffers provide thread-safe audio data transfer
  • Real-time priority scheduling on supported platforms

PLATFORM SUPPORT:

  • Windows: DirectSound, WASAPI, or ASIO backends
  • Linux: ALSA backend only (JACK explicitly disabled)
  • macOS: CoreAudio backend

Note

Audio capture and playback can be started/stopped independently.

The audio system uses ring buffers to smooth out timing variations between capture/playback threads and network I/O.

Real-time priority is automatically requested on supported platforms to minimize audio glitches and latency.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

October 2025

This header provides professional-quality audio mixing for ascii-chat's multi-client audio chat functionality. The mixer combines audio from multiple clients with advanced processing including compression, ducking, noise gating, and high-pass filtering.

CORE FEATURES:

  • Multi-source audio mixing (up to 10 simultaneous sources)
  • Active speaker detection and ducking
  • Dynamic range compression
  • Noise gate with hysteresis
  • High-pass filtering for noise reduction
  • Crowd scaling (automatic volume adjustment based on participant count)
  • Optimized O(1) source exclusion using bitsets
  • Reader-writer lock synchronization for concurrent access

AUDIO PROCESSING PIPELINE:

The mixer processes audio through the following stages:

1.
Source Reading: Reads samples from client audio ring buffers
2.
Ducking: Identifies active speaker and attenuates background sources
3.
Mixing: Combines all active sources with crowd scaling
4.
Compression: Applies dynamic range compression to prevent clipping
5.
Noise Gate: Suppresses background noise below threshold
6.
High-Pass Filter: Removes low-frequency noise and rumble
7.
Soft Clipping: Prevents hard clipping artifacts

DUCKING SYSTEM:

The ducking system automatically:

  • Detects the loudest active speaker
  • Identifies sources within a margin of the loudest (also considered active)
  • Attenuates non-active sources to prevent echo and feedback
  • Uses smooth attack/release curves for natural transitions

COMPRESSION:

The compressor provides:

  • Threshold-based compression (acts above threshold)
  • Configurable compression ratio (e.g., 4:1)
  • Soft knee for smooth compression curves
  • Independent attack and release times
  • Automatic makeup gain compensation

OPTIMIZATIONS:

  • Bitset-based active source tracking (O(1) exclusion vs O(n))
  • Hash table for client ID to mixer index mapping
  • Reader-writer locks for concurrent source management
  • Pre-allocated mix buffer to avoid allocations in hot path

THREAD SAFETY:

  • Source arrays protected by reader-writer locks
  • Bitset operations are atomic
  • Mix processing is thread-safe once sources are locked

Note

The mixer processes audio in fixed-size frames (256 samples) for consistent latency and processing behavior.

Ducking parameters should be tuned based on room acoustics and expected participant count for optimal performance.

Crowd scaling automatically reduces per-source volume as more participants join, preventing audio overload.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

October 2025

Provides high-level interface to libopus for encoding and decoding audio frames with configurable bitrate and frame sizes.

OPUS CODEC FEATURES:

  • Real-time audio compression with minimal latency
  • Flexible bitrate from 6 kbps (voice) to 128+ kbps (music)
  • Adaptive frame sizes (10ms, 20ms, 40ms, 60ms)
  • Automatic detection of voice vs music content
  • Graceful handling of packet loss

USAGE EXAMPLE: 

// Create encoder for voice at 24 kbps
opus_codec_t *encoder = opus_codec_create_encoder(OPUS_APPLICATION_VOIP, 44100, 24000);
// Encode audio (882 samples = 20ms at 44.1kHz)
float samples[882];
uint8_t compressed[250];
size_t encoded_bytes = opus_codec_encode(encoder, samples, 882, compressed, 250);
// Send over network...
// Create decoder
opus_codec_t *decoder = opus_codec_create_decoder(44100);
// Decode received audio
float decoded[882];
int decoded_samples = opus_codec_decode(decoder, compressed, encoded_bytes, decoded, 882);
// Cleanup
opus_codec_destroy(encoder);
opus_codec_destroy(decoder);

Note

Thread-safety: Each codec instance must not be accessed from multiple threads simultaneously. Create separate encoder and decoder instances per thread if needed.

Frame sizes: Opus works with fixed frame sizes. Recommended:

  • 20ms (882 samples @ 44.1kHz) for voice
  • 40ms (1764 samples @ 44.1kHz) for low-latency music

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

December 2025

asciichat_error_t audio_dequantize_samples (const
  uint8_t * samples_ptr, uint32_t total_samples, float *
  out_samples)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/audio/audio.h>


Dequantize network audio samples from int32 to float.


Parameters


samples_ptr Pointer to quantized samples (network
  byte order uint32_t values)


total_samples Number of samples to convert


out_samples Output buffer for float samples (must be pre-allocated)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Converts quantized audio samples from network byte order
    (uint32_t) to floating point format. Performs the following
    transformation:


    
  
  • Read uint32_t in network byte order
    • 
  
  • Convert to int32_t via ntohl()
    • 
  
  • Scale to float range [-1.0, 1.0] by dividing by 2147483647.0
    • 



This helper eliminates code duplication between server and client
    handlers.




Note


out_samples must be pre-allocated by caller with space
  for total_samples floats



samples_ptr should point to total_samples * sizeof(uint32_t)
  bytes




Definition at line 1978 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


1978                                                                                                                    {
1979   if (!samples_ptr || !out_samples || total_samples == 0) {
1980     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters for audio dequantization");
1981   }
1982 
1983   for (uint32_t i = 0; i < total_samples; i++) {
1984     uint32_t network_sample;
1985     // Use memcpy to safely handle potential misalignment from packet header
1986     memcpy(&network_sample, samples_ptr + i * sizeof(uint32_t), sizeof(uint32_t));
1987     int32_t scaled = (int32_t)NET_TO_HOST_U32(network_sample);
1988     out_samples[i] = (float)scaled / 2147483647.0f;
1989   }
1990 
1991   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1992 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    NET_TO_HOST_U32, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by handle_audio_batch_packet().

  audio_destroy (audio_context_t * ctx)

  <include/ascii-chat/audio/audio.h>


Destroy audio context and clean up resources.


Parameters


ctx Audio context to destroy (can be NULL)


Stops all audio streams, destroys ring buffers, and cleans up
    PortAudio resources. Safe to call multiple times or with NULL pointer.




Note


Automatically stops capture and playback if active.



Ring buffers are destroyed and all data is lost.




PortAudio is terminated after last context is destroyed.

1289   if (!ctx) {
1290     return;

1293   // Always release PortAudio refcount if it was incremented
1294   // audio_init() calls Pa_Initialize() very early, and if it fails partway through,
1295   // ctx->initialized will be false. But we MUST still call audio_release_portaudio()
1296   // to properly decrement the refcount and allow Pa_Terminate() to be called.
1297   if (ctx->initialized) {
1298 
1299     // Stop duplex stream if running (this also stops the worker thread)
1300     if (ctx->running) {
1301       audio_stop_duplex(ctx);
1302     }
1303 
1304     // Ensure worker thread is stopped even if streams weren't running
1305     if (ctx->worker_running) {
1306       log_debug("Stopping worker thread during audio_destroy");
1307       atomic_store_bool(&ctx->worker_should_stop, true);
1308       cond_signal(&ctx->worker_cond); // Wake up worker if waiting
1309       asciichat_thread_join(&ctx->worker_thread, NULL);
1310       ctx->worker_running = false;
1311     }
1312 
1313     mutex_lock(&ctx->state_mutex);
1314 
1315     // Destroy all ring buffers (old + new)

1317     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(ctx->playback_buffer);

1319     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(ctx->raw_render_rb);

1322

1325     SAFE_FREE(ctx->worker_render_batch);
1326     SAFE_FREE(ctx->worker_playback_batch);
1327

1333 
1334     mutex_unlock(&ctx->state_mutex);

Flush all audio buffers to synchronize with seek operations.


Parameters

Initialize audio context and PortAudio.


Parameters


ctx Audio context to initialize (must not be
  NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Initializes the audio context and PortAudio library. Creates ring
    buffers for capture and playback but does not start streams. Must be called
    before any other audio functions.




Note


PortAudio initialization is idempotent and thread-safe.

Use audio_start_duplex() to begin full-duplex audio

Must call audio_destroy() to clean up resources

  sample_rate)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/audio/audio.h>


Check if a sample rate is a standard/supported rate. Supported
    rates: 8000, 16000, 24000, 32000, 44100, 48000, 96000, 192000




Parameters


sample_rate Sample rate in Hz


Returns


true if the sample rate is supported, false
  otherwise


Usage:




if (!audio_is_supported_sample_rate(batch.sample_rate)) {


    log_error("Unsupported sample rate: %u", batch.sample_rate);


    return;
}



Definition at line 2074 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


2074                                                           {
2075   // List of commonly supported audio sample rates
2076   static const uint32_t supported_rates[] = {
2077       8000,   // Telephone quality
2078       16000,  // Wideband telephony
2079       24000,  // High quality speech
2080       32000,  // Good for video
2081       44100,  // CD quality (less common in VoIP)
2082       48000,  // Standard professional
2083       96000,  // High-end professional
2084       192000, // Ultra-high-end mastering
2085   };
2086 
2087   const size_t rate_count = sizeof(supported_rates) / sizeof(supported_rates[0]);
2088   for (size_t i = 0; i < rate_count; i++) {
2089     if (sample_rate == supported_rates[i]) {
2090       return true;
2091     }
2092   }
2093 
2094   return false;
2095 }

asciichat_error_t audio_parse_batch_header (const void *
  data, size_t len, audio_batch_info_t * out_batch)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/audio/audio.h>


Parse an audio batch packet header from raw packet data. Performs
    the following validations:



  
1.

  
Checks that the data pointer is not NULL

  
2.

  
Checks that len is at least sizeof(audio_batch_header_t)

  
3.

  
Verifies that batch_count and channels are within reasonable bounds

  
4.

  
Unpacks all 32-bit values from network byte order to host byte order




Parameters


data Pointer to packet data (must not be NULL)


len Length of packet data in bytes


out_batch Pointer to audio_batch_info_t struct to fill with parsed
  data


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK if parsing succeeded



ERROR_INVALID_PARAM if data is NULL or out_batch is NULL




ERROR_INVALID_PARAM if len is too small for the header




Usage:




audio_batch_info_t batch;
asciichat_error_t result = audio_parse_batch_header(packet_data, packet_len, &batch);
if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {


    log_error("Failed to parse audio batch: %d", result);


    return;
}
// Now batch.batch_count, batch.sample_rate, etc. are ready to use
log_debug("Audio batch: %u frames at %u Hz, %u channels",


          batch.batch_count, batch.sample_rate, batch.channels);



Definition at line 2008 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


2008                                                                                                         {
2009   if (!data) {
2010     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch header data pointer is NULL");
2011   }
2012 
2013   if (!out_batch) {
2014     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch info output pointer is NULL");
2015   }
2016 
2017   if (len < sizeof(audio_batch_packet_t)) {
2018     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch header too small (len=%zu, expected=%zu)", len,
2019                      sizeof(audio_batch_packet_t));
2020   }
2021 
2022   const audio_batch_packet_t *batch_header = (const audio_batch_packet_t *)data;
2023 
2024   // Unpack network byte order values to host byte order
2025   out_batch->batch_count = ntohl(batch_header->batch_count);
2026   out_batch->total_samples = ntohl(batch_header->total_samples);
2027   out_batch->sample_rate = ntohl(batch_header->sample_rate);
2028   out_batch->channels = ntohl(batch_header->channels);
2029 
2030   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2031 }

asciichat_error_t audio_read_samples
  (audio_context_t * ctx, float * buffer, int num_samples)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/audio/audio.h>


Read captured audio samples from capture buffer.


Parameters


ctx Audio context (must not be NULL)


buffer Output buffer for audio samples (must not be NULL)


num_samples Number of samples to read (must be > 0)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Reads audio samples from the capture ring buffer. Samples are in
    floating point format (-1.0 to 1.0 range). This function is non-blocking and
    returns whatever samples are available up to num_samples.




Note


Actual number of samples read may be less than
  num_samples if buffer doesn't contain enough samples.



Samples are removed from the buffer after reading.




Thread-safe: Can be called from any thread.




Warning


Buffer must be large enough to hold num_samples float
  values.

1861                      num_samples);
1862   }
1863 
1864   // audio_ring_buffer_read now returns number of samples read, not error code
1865   int samples_read = audio_ring_buffer_read(ctx->capture_buffer, buffer, num_samples);
1866   return (samples_read >= 0) ? ASCIICHAT_OK : ERROR_AUDIO;

  audio_ring_buffer_available_read (audio_ring_buffer_t * rb)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/audio/audio.h>


Get number of samples available for reading.


Parameters


rb Audio ring buffer (must not be NULL)


Returns


Number of samples available to read


Returns the current number of samples available in the ring buffer
    for reading. Useful for determining if enough samples are available before
    calling audio_ring_buffer_read().




Note


Thread-safe: Can be called from any thread.



Value may change immediately after return due to concurrent
    writes.

1111                                                                  {
1112   if (!rb)
1113     return 0;
1114 
1115   // LOCK-FREE: Load indices with proper memory ordering
1116   // Use acquire for write_index to see writer's updates
1117   // Use relaxed for read_index (our own index)
1118   unsigned int write_idx = atomic_load_u64(&rb->write_index);
1119   unsigned int read_idx = atomic_load_u64(&rb->read_index);
1120 
1121   if (write_idx >= read_idx) {
1122     return write_idx - read_idx;
1123   }
1124 
1125   return AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - read_idx + write_idx;

849     return;

852   // Reset buffer to empty state (no audio to play = silence at shutdown)
853   atomic_store_u64(&rb->write_index, 0);
854   atomic_store_u64(&rb->read_index, 0);
855   rb->last_sample = 0.0f;

#include
  <include/ascii-chat/audio/audio.h>


Create a new audio ring buffer (for playback with jitter
    buffering).


Returns


Pointer to newly created audio ring buffer, or NULL on
  failure


Creates a new audio ring buffer for storing audio samples. The
    buffer has a fixed size defined by AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE and is optimized
    for real-time audio streaming with jitter buffering support (enabled by
    default).




Note


The ring buffer is thread-safe and can be used
  concurrently by capture and playback threads.



Ring buffer includes jitter buffer threshold for network latency
    compensation.




Use audio_ring_buffer_create_for_capture() for capture
    buffers.




Warning


Must call audio_ring_buffer_destroy() to free
  resources.

#include
  <include/ascii-chat/audio/audio.h>


Create a new audio ring buffer for capture (without jitter
    buffering).


Returns


Pointer to newly created audio ring buffer, or NULL on
  failure


Creates an audio ring buffer optimized for direct microphone input
    from PortAudio. Unlike playback buffers, capture buffers disable jitter
    buffering since PortAudio writes directly from the microphone with no
    network latency.




Note


The ring buffer is thread-safe and can be used
  concurrently by capture and playback threads.



Jitter buffering is disabled for capture buffers.




Warning

#include

    signal). This function reads samples from the jitter buffer WITHOUT
    advancing the read_index. Used to feed audio to AEC3 for echo cancellation

1076     return 0;
1077   }
1078 
1079   // LOCK-FREE: Load indices with proper memory ordering
1080   unsigned int write_idx = atomic_load_u64(&rb->write_index);
1081   unsigned int read_idx = atomic_load_u64(&rb->read_index);
1082 
1083   // Calculate available samples
1084   size_t available;
1085   if (write_idx >= read_idx) {
1086     available = write_idx - read_idx;
1087   } else {
1088     available = AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - read_idx + write_idx;
1089   }
1090 
1091   size_t to_peek = (samples > available) ? available : samples;
1092 
1093   if (to_peek == 0) {
1094     return 0;
1095   }
1096 
1097   // Copy samples in chunks (handle wraparound)
1098   size_t first_chunk = (read_idx + to_peek <= AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE) ? to_peek : (AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - read_idx);
1099 
1100   SAFE_MEMCPY(data, first_chunk * sizeof(float), rb->data + read_idx, first_chunk * sizeof(float));
1101 
1102   if (first_chunk < to_peek) {
1103     // Wraparound: copy second chunk from beginning of buffer
1104     size_t second_chunk = to_peek - first_chunk;
1105     SAFE_MEMCPY(data + first_chunk, second_chunk * sizeof(float), rb->data, second_chunk * sizeof(float));
1106   }
1107 
1108   return to_peek;
1109 }

size_t
  audio_ring_buffer_read (audio_ring_buffer_t * rb, float * data, size_t
  samples)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/audio/audio.h>


Read audio samples from ring buffer.


Parameters


rb Audio ring buffer (must not be NULL)


data Output buffer for audio samples (must not be NULL)


samples Maximum number of samples to read


Returns


Number of samples actually read (may be less than
  requested)


Reads audio samples from the ring buffer. Samples are in floating
    point format (-1.0 to 1.0 range). This function is non-blocking and returns
    whatever samples are available up to the requested count.




Note


Actual number of samples read may be less than samples if
  buffer doesn't contain enough samples.



Samples are removed from the buffer after reading.




Thread-safe: Can be called from multiple threads
  simultaneously.




Warning


Data buffer must be large enough to hold samples float
  values.


Definition at line 944 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


944                                                                                     {
945   if (!rb || !data || samples <= 0) {
946     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: rb=%p, data=%p, samples=%d", rb, data, samples);
947     return 0; // Return 0 samples read on error
948   }
949 
950   // LOCK-FREE: Load indices with proper memory ordering
951   // - Load writer's write_index with acquire (see writer's data updates)
952   // - Load our own read_index with relaxed (no sync needed with ourselves)
953   unsigned int write_idx = atomic_load_u64(&rb->write_index);
954   unsigned int read_idx = atomic_load_u64(&rb->read_index);
955 
956   // Calculate available samples
957   size_t available;
958   if (write_idx >= read_idx) {
959     available = write_idx - read_idx;
960   } else {
961     available = AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - read_idx + write_idx;
962   }
963 
964   // LOCK-FREE: Load jitter buffer state with acquire ordering
965   bool jitter_filled = atomic_load_u64(&rb->jitter_buffer_filled);
966   int crossfade_remaining = atomic_load_u64(&rb->crossfade_samples_remaining);
967   bool fade_in = atomic_load_u64(&rb->crossfade_fade_in);
968 
969   // Jitter buffer: don't read until initial fill threshold is reached
970   // (only for playback buffers - capture buffers have jitter_buffer_enabled = false)
971   if (!jitter_filled && rb->jitter_buffer_enabled) {
972     // Check if we've accumulated enough samples to start playback
973     if (available >= AUDIO_JITTER_BUFFER_THRESHOLD) {
974       atomic_store_u64(&rb->jitter_buffer_filled, true);
975       atomic_store_u64(&rb->crossfade_samples_remaining, AUDIO_CROSSFADE_SAMPLES);
976       atomic_store_u64(&rb->crossfade_fade_in, true);
977       log_info("Jitter buffer filled (%zu samples), starting playback with fade-in", available);
978       // Reload state for processing below
979       jitter_filled = true;
980       crossfade_remaining = AUDIO_CROSSFADE_SAMPLES;
981       fade_in = true;
982     } else {
983       // Log buffer fill progress every second
984       log_debug_every(NS_PER_MS_INT, "Jitter buffer filling: %zu/%d samples (%.1f%%)", available,
985                       AUDIO_JITTER_BUFFER_THRESHOLD, (100.0f * available) / AUDIO_JITTER_BUFFER_THRESHOLD);
986       return 0; // Return 0 samples - caller will pad with silence
987     }
988   }
989 
990   // Periodic buffer health logging (every 5 seconds when healthy)
991   unsigned int underruns = atomic_load_u64(&rb->underrun_count);
992   log_dev_every(5 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "Buffer health: %zu/%d samples (%.1f%%), underruns=%u", available,
993                 AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE, (100.0f * available) / AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE, underruns);
994 
995   // Low buffer handling: DON'T pause playback - continue reading what's available
996   // and fill the rest with silence. Pausing causes a feedback loop where:
997   // 1. Underrun -> pause reading -> buffer overflows from incoming samples
998   // 2. Threshold reached -> resume reading -> drains too fast -> underrun again
999   //
1000   // Instead: always consume samples to prevent overflow, use silence for missing data
1001   if (rb->jitter_buffer_enabled && available < AUDIO_JITTER_LOW_WATER_MARK) {
1002     unsigned int underrun_count = atomic_fetch_add_u64(&rb->underrun_count, 1) + 1;
1003     log_warn_every(LOG_RATE_FAST,
1004                    "Audio buffer low #%u: only %zu samples available (low water mark: %d), padding with silence",
1005                    underrun_count, available, AUDIO_JITTER_LOW_WATER_MARK);
1006     // Don't set jitter_buffer_filled = false - keep reading to prevent overflow
1007   }
1008 
1009   size_t to_read = (samples > available) ? available : samples;
1010 
1011   // Optimize: copy in chunks instead of one sample at a time
1012   size_t remaining = AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - read_idx;
1013 
1014   if (to_read <= remaining) {
1015     // Can copy in one chunk
1016     SAFE_MEMCPY(data, to_read * sizeof(float), &rb->data[read_idx], to_read * sizeof(float));
1017   } else {
1018     // Need to wrap around - copy in two chunks
1019     SAFE_MEMCPY(data, remaining * sizeof(float), &rb->data[read_idx], remaining * sizeof(float));
1020     SAFE_MEMCPY(&data[remaining], (to_read - remaining) * sizeof(float), &rb->data[0],
1021                 (to_read - remaining) * sizeof(float));
1022   }
1023 
1024   // LOCK-FREE: Store new read_index with release ordering
1025   // This ensures all data reads above complete before the index update
1026   unsigned int new_read_idx = (read_idx + (unsigned int)to_read) % AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE;
1027   atomic_store_u64(&rb->read_index, new_read_idx);
1028 
1029   // Apply fade-in if recovering from underrun
1030   if (fade_in && crossfade_remaining > 0) {
1031     int fade_start = AUDIO_CROSSFADE_SAMPLES - crossfade_remaining;
1032     size_t fade_samples = (to_read < (size_t)crossfade_remaining) ? to_read : (size_t)crossfade_remaining;
1033 
1034     for (size_t i = 0; i < fade_samples; i++) {
1035       float fade_factor = (float)(fade_start + (int)i + 1) / (float)AUDIO_CROSSFADE_SAMPLES;
1036       data[i] *= fade_factor;
1037     }
1038 
1039     int new_crossfade_remaining = crossfade_remaining - (int)fade_samples;
1040     atomic_store_u64(&rb->crossfade_samples_remaining, new_crossfade_remaining);
1041     if (new_crossfade_remaining <= 0) {
1042       atomic_store_u64(&rb->crossfade_fade_in, false);
1043       log_debug("Audio fade-in complete");
1044     }
1045   }
1046 
1047   // Save last sample for potential fade-out
1048   // Note: only update if we actually read some data
1049   // This is NOT atomic - only the reader thread writes this
1050   if (to_read > 0) {
1051     rb->last_sample = data[to_read - 1];
1052   }
1053 
1054   // Return ACTUAL number of samples read, not padded count
1055   // The caller (mixer) expects truthful return values to detect underruns
1056   // and handle silence padding externally. Internal padding creates double-padding bugs.
1057   //
1058   // This function was incorrectly returning `samples` even when
1059   // it only read `to_read` samples. This broke the mixer's underrun detection.
1060   return to_read;
1061 }

asciichat_error_t audio_ring_buffer_write
  (audio_ring_buffer_t * rb, const float * data, int samples)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/audio/audio.h>


Write audio samples to ring buffer.


Parameters


rb Audio ring buffer (must not be NULL)


data Audio samples to write (must not be NULL)


samples Number of samples to write


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Writes audio samples to the ring buffer. Samples should be in
    floating point format (-1.0 to 1.0 range). This function is non-blocking and
    writes as many samples as buffer space allows.




Note


Actual number of samples written may be less than
  requested if buffer is full or nearly full.



Thread-safe: Can be called from multiple threads
  simultaneously.




Warning


Data must contain at least samples float values.


Definition at line 861 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


861                                                                                                    {
862   if (!rb || !data || samples <= 0)
863     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: rb=%p, data=%p, samples=%d", rb, data, samples);
864 
865   // Validate samples doesn't exceed our buffer size
866   if (samples > AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE) {
867     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER, "Attempted to write %d samples, but buffer size is only %d", samples,
868                      AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE);
869   }
870 
871   // LOCK-FREE: Load indices with proper memory ordering
872   // - Load our own write_index with relaxed (no sync needed with ourselves)
873   // - Load reader's read_index with acquire (see reader's updates to free space)
874   unsigned int write_idx = atomic_load_u64(&rb->write_index);
875   unsigned int read_idx = atomic_load_u64(&rb->read_index);
876 
877   // Calculate current buffer level (how many samples are buffered)
878   int buffer_level;
879   if (write_idx >= read_idx) {
880     buffer_level = (int)(write_idx - read_idx);
881   } else {
882     buffer_level = AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - (int)(read_idx - write_idx);
883   }
884   // Reserve 1 slot to distinguish between full and empty states
885   // (when buffer is full, write_idx will be just before read_idx, not equal to it)
886   int available = AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - 1 - buffer_level;
887 
888   // HIGH WATER MARK: Drop INCOMING samples to prevent latency accumulation
889   // Writer must not modify read_index (race condition with reader).
890   // Instead, we drop incoming samples to keep buffer bounded.
891   // This sacrifices newest data to prevent unbounded latency growth.
892   if (buffer_level > AUDIO_JITTER_HIGH_WATER_MARK) {
893     // Buffer is already too full - drop incoming samples to maintain target level
894     int target_writes = AUDIO_JITTER_TARGET_LEVEL - buffer_level;
895     if (target_writes < 0) {
896       target_writes = 0; // Buffer is way over - drop everything
897     }
898 
899     if (samples > target_writes) {
900       int dropped = samples - target_writes;
901       log_warn_every(LOG_RATE_FAST,
902                      "Audio buffer high water mark exceeded (%d > %d): dropping %d INCOMING samples "
903                      "(keeping newest %d to maintain target %d)",
904                      buffer_level, AUDIO_JITTER_HIGH_WATER_MARK, dropped, target_writes, AUDIO_JITTER_TARGET_LEVEL);
905       samples = target_writes; // Only write what fits within target level
906     }
907   }
908 
909   // Now write the new samples - should always have enough space after above
910   int samples_to_write = samples;
911   if (samples > available) {
912     // This should rarely happen after the high water mark logic above
913     int samples_dropped = samples - available;
914     samples_to_write = available;
915     log_warn_every(LOG_RATE_FAST, "Audio buffer overflow: dropping %d of %d incoming samples (buffer_used=%d/%d)",
916                    samples_dropped, samples, AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - available, AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE);
917   }
918 
919   // Write only the samples that fit (preserves existing data integrity)
920   if (samples_to_write > 0) {
921     int remaining = AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - (int)write_idx;
922 
923     if (samples_to_write <= remaining) {
924       // Can copy in one chunk
925       SAFE_MEMCPY(&rb->data[write_idx], samples_to_write * sizeof(float), data, samples_to_write * sizeof(float));
926     } else {
927       // Need to wrap around - copy in two chunks
928       SAFE_MEMCPY(&rb->data[write_idx], remaining * sizeof(float), data, remaining * sizeof(float));
929       SAFE_MEMCPY(&rb->data[0], (samples_to_write - remaining) * sizeof(float), &data[remaining],
930                   (samples_to_write - remaining) * sizeof(float));
931     }
932 
933     // LOCK-FREE: Store new write_index with release ordering
934     // This ensures all data writes above are visible before the index update
935     unsigned int new_write_idx = (write_idx + (unsigned int)samples_to_write) % AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE;
936     atomic_store_u64(&rb->write_index, new_write_idx);
937   }
938 
939   // Note: jitter buffer fill check is now done in read function for better control
940 
941   return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Success
942 }

    AUDIO_JITTER_TARGET_LEVEL, AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE,
    audio_ring_buffer::data, ERROR_BUFFER,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, LOG_RATE_FAST, log_warn_every,
    audio_ring_buffer::read_index, SAFE_MEMCPY, SET_ERRNO,

void
  audio_set_pipeline (audio_context_t * ctx, void * pipeline)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/audio/audio.h>


Set audio pipeline for echo cancellation.


Parameters


ctx Audio context (must not be NULL)


pipeline Client audio pipeline pointer (opaque, can be NULL)


Associates an audio pipeline with the context for echo
    cancellation. The pipeline is fed playback samples from the output callback,
    allowing AEC3 to properly synchronize render and capture signals.




Note


Safe to call at any time, including before/after audio
  streams are started.



Pass NULL to disable pipeline integration.

  lib/audio/audio.c.


1342                                                               {
1343   if (!ctx)
1344     return;
1345   ctx->audio_pipeline = pipeline;
1346 }






References audio_context_t::audio_pipeline.


Referenced by audio_cleanup(), and
    audio_client_init().






asciichat_error_t audio_set_realtime_priority (void )


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/audio/audio.h>


Request real-time priority for current thread.


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Requests real-time priority scheduling for the current thread.
    This reduces audio latency and glitches by ensuring audio threads get
    scheduled promptly. Platform-specific implementation (SCHED_FIFO on Linux,
    thread priority on Windows/macOS).




Note


This function should be called from audio
  capture/playback threads.



On some platforms, real-time priority requires appropriate
    permissions.




If real-time priority cannot be obtained, the function may succeed
    but use a lower priority level.




Warning


Real-time priority can cause system instability if
  misused. Only use in audio threads that process data quickly.



LIMITATION: This function only affects the calling thread. When
    used with PortAudio, the audio callbacks run in PortAudio's internal
    threads, not the thread that calls audio_start_duplex. Some PortAudio
    backends (WASAPI, CoreAudio) automatically use real-time priority for their
    callback threads. For other backends, this function has limited effect when
    called from the main thread.




Definition at line 1994 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


1994                                                     {
1995   // Delegate to platform abstraction layer
1996   asciichat_error_t result = asciichat_thread_set_realtime_priority();
1997   if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1998     log_debug("✓ Audio thread real-time priority set successfully");
1999   }
2000   return result;
2001 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_thread_set_realtime_priority(), and log_debug.


Referenced by audio_start_duplex().






bool audio_should_enable_microphone (audio_source_t
  source, bool has_media_audio)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/audio/audio.h>


Determine if microphone should be enabled based on audio source
    setting and media state. Smart helper function for determining microphone
    input availability based on:


    
  
  • User's --audio-source preference (auto, microphone, media, both)
    • 
  
  • Whether media audio is currently being played (--file or --url)
    • 



Parameters


source Audio source preference from --audio-source
  option


has_media_audio True if media with audio is being played, false
  otherwise


Returns


true if microphone should be enabled, false
  otherwise


Behavior by audio_source value:


    
  
  • AUDIO_SOURCE_AUTO: Enable microphone only when no media audio (smart
      default)
    • 
  
  • AUDIO_SOURCE_MICROPHONE: Always enable microphone (ignore media
    state)
    • 
  
  • AUDIO_SOURCE_MEDIA: Never enable microphone (media-only)
    • 
  
  • AUDIO_SOURCE_BOTH: Always enable microphone (allow simultaneous
    capture)
    • 



Usage:




bool has_media = (media_file && strlen(media_file) > 0);
bool enable_mic = audio_should_enable_microphone(GET_OPTION(audio_source), has_media);
if (enable_mic) {


    audio_ctx->playback_only = false;  // Allow microphone capture
} else {


    audio_ctx->playback_only = true;   // Disable microphone to prevent interference
}



Definition at line 2097 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


2097                                                                                  {
2098   switch (source) {
2099   case AUDIO_SOURCE_AUTO:
2100     return !has_media_audio;
2101 
2102   case AUDIO_SOURCE_MIC:
2103     return true;
2104 
2105   case AUDIO_SOURCE_MEDIA:
2106     return false;
2107 
2108   case AUDIO_SOURCE_BOTH:
2109     return true;
2110 
2111   default:
2112     return !has_media_audio;
2113   }
2114 }






References AUDIO_SOURCE_AUTO, AUDIO_SOURCE_BOTH,
    AUDIO_SOURCE_MEDIA, and AUDIO_SOURCE_MIC.


Referenced by session_client_like_run().






asciichat_error_t audio_start_duplex
  (audio_context_t * ctx)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/audio/audio.h>


Start full-duplex audio (simultaneous capture and playback).


Parameters


ctx Audio context (must not be NULL, must be
  initialized)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Opens a single PortAudio stream that handles BOTH input
    (microphone) and output (speakers) simultaneously. This is CRITICAL for
    proper AEC3 echo cancellation because:




    
  
  • Single callback receives both render and capture samples at the EXACT same
      instant
    • 
  
  • No timing mismatch between input/output callbacks
    • 
  
  • No ring buffer delay for AEC3 reference signal
    • 
  
  • Perfect synchronization for echo cancellation
    • 



The duplex callback:



  
1.

  
Reads from playback_buffer → outputs to speakers

  
2.

  
Processes capture through AEC3 (using render as reference)

  
3.

  
Writes processed capture to capture_buffer for encoder thread




Note


Real-time priority is automatically requested for
  callback thread.



If already running, this function has no effect.




Warning


Both input and output devices must be available.


Definition at line 1367 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


1367                                                            {
1368   if (!ctx || !ctx->initialized) {
1369     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Audio context not initialized");
1370   }
1371 
1372   // Initialize PortAudio here, when we actually need to open streams
1373   // This defers Pa_Initialize() until necessary, avoiding premature ALSA allocation
1374   asciichat_error_t pa_result = audio_ensure_portaudio_initialized();
1375   if (pa_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1376     return pa_result;
1377   }
1378 
1379   // Check if already running (without holding lock during blocking operations)
1380   // Do this check first before acquiring any locks
1381   if (ctx->duplex_stream || ctx->input_stream || ctx->output_stream) {
1382     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1383   }
1384 
1385   // Setup input parameters (skip if playback-only mode)
1386   PaStreamParameters inputParams = {0};
1387   const PaDeviceInfo *inputInfo = NULL;
1388   bool has_input = false;
1389 
1390   if (!ctx->playback_only) {
1391     if (GET_OPTION(microphone_index) >= 0) {
1392       inputParams.device = GET_OPTION(microphone_index);
1393     } else {
1394       inputParams.device = Pa_GetDefaultInputDevice();
1395     }
1396 
1397     if (inputParams.device == paNoDevice) {
1398       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_AUDIO, "No input device available");
1399     }
1400 
1401     inputInfo = Pa_GetDeviceInfo(inputParams.device);
1402     if (!inputInfo) {
1403       mutex_unlock(&ctx->state_mutex);
1404       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_AUDIO, "Input device info not found");
1405     }
1406 
1407     has_input = true;
1408     inputParams.channelCount = AUDIO_CHANNELS;
1409     inputParams.sampleFormat = paFloat32;
1410     inputParams.suggestedLatency = inputInfo->defaultLowInputLatency;
1411     inputParams.hostApiSpecificStreamInfo = NULL;
1412   }
1413 
1414   // Setup output parameters
1415   PaStreamParameters outputParams;
1416   const PaDeviceInfo *outputInfo = NULL;
1417   bool has_output = false;
1418 
1419   if (GET_OPTION(speakers_index) >= 0) {
1420     outputParams.device = GET_OPTION(speakers_index);
1421   } else {
1422     outputParams.device = Pa_GetDefaultOutputDevice();
1423   }
1424 
1425   if (outputParams.device != paNoDevice) {
1426     outputInfo = Pa_GetDeviceInfo(outputParams.device);
1427     if (outputInfo) {
1428       has_output = true;
1429       outputParams.channelCount = AUDIO_CHANNELS;
1430       outputParams.sampleFormat = paFloat32;
1431       outputParams.suggestedLatency = outputInfo->defaultLowOutputLatency;
1432       outputParams.hostApiSpecificStreamInfo = NULL;
1433     } else {
1434       log_warn("Output device info not found for device %d", outputParams.device);
1435     }
1436   }
1437 
1438   // Store device rates for diagnostics (only access if device info was retrieved)
1439   ctx->input_device_rate = (has_input && inputInfo) ? inputInfo->defaultSampleRate : 0;
1440   ctx->output_device_rate = (has_output && outputInfo) ? outputInfo->defaultSampleRate : 0;
1441 
1442   log_debug("Opening audio:");
1443   if (has_input) {
1444     log_info("  Input:  %s (%.0f Hz)", inputInfo->name, inputInfo->defaultSampleRate);
1445   } else if (ctx->playback_only) {
1446     log_debug("  Input:  (playback-only mode - no microphone)");
1447   } else {
1448     log_debug("  Input:  (none)");
1449   }
1450   if (has_output) {
1451     log_info("  Output: %s (%.0f Hz)", outputInfo->name, outputInfo->defaultSampleRate);
1452   } else {
1453     log_debug("  Output: None (input-only mode - will send audio to server)");
1454   }
1455 
1456   // Check if sample rates differ - ALSA full-duplex doesn't handle this well
1457   // If no input or no output, always use separate streams
1458   bool rates_differ = has_input && has_output && (inputInfo->defaultSampleRate != outputInfo->defaultSampleRate);
1459   bool try_separate = rates_differ || !has_input || !has_output;
1460   PaError err = paNoError;
1461 
1462   if (!try_separate) {
1463     // Try full-duplex first (preferred - perfect AEC3 timing)
1464     LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1465       err = Pa_OpenStream(&ctx->duplex_stream, &inputParams, &outputParams, AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE, AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER,
1466                           paClipOff, duplex_callback, ctx);
1467     });
1468 
1469     if (err == paNoError) {
1470       LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1471         err = Pa_StartStream(ctx->duplex_stream);
1472       });
1473       if (err != paNoError) {
1474         LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1475           Pa_CloseStream(ctx->duplex_stream);
1476         });
1477         ctx->duplex_stream = NULL;
1478         log_warn("Full-duplex stream failed to start: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1479         try_separate = true;
1480       }
1481     } else {
1482       log_warn("Full-duplex stream failed to open: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1483       try_separate = true;
1484     }
1485   }
1486 
1487   if (try_separate) {
1488     // Fall back to separate streams (needed when sample rates differ or input-only/playback-only mode)
1489     if (has_output && has_input) {
1490       log_info("Using separate input/output streams (sample rates differ: %.0f vs %.0f Hz)",
1491                inputInfo->defaultSampleRate, outputInfo->defaultSampleRate);
1492       log_info("  Will resample: buffer at %.0f Hz → output at %.0f Hz", (double)AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE,
1493                outputInfo->defaultSampleRate);
1494     } else if (has_output) {
1495       log_debug("Using output-only mode (playback-only for mirror/media)");
1496     } else if (has_input) {
1497       log_info("Using input-only mode (no output device available)");
1498     }
1499 
1500     // Store the internal sample rate (buffer rate)
1501     ctx->sample_rate = AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE;
1502 
1503     // Create render buffer for AEC3 reference synchronization
1504     ctx->render_buffer = audio_ring_buffer_create_for_capture();
1505     if (!ctx->render_buffer) {
1506       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create render buffer");
1507     }
1508 
1509     // Open output stream only if output device exists
1510     bool output_ok = false;
1511     double actual_output_rate = 0;
1512     if (has_output) {
1513       // Try to use AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE (48kHz) first for best quality and duplex compatibility
1514       // Fall back to native rate if 48kHz not supported
1515       double preferred_rate = AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE;
1516       double native_rate = outputInfo->defaultSampleRate;
1517 
1518       log_debug("Attempting output at %.0f Hz (preferred) vs %.0f Hz (native)", preferred_rate, native_rate);
1519 
1520       // Always use output_callback for output streams (both output-only and duplex modes)
1521       // PortAudio will invoke the callback whenever it needs audio data
1522       // The callback reads from media_source (mirror mode) or playback buffers (network mode)
1523       PaStreamCallback *callback = output_callback;
1524 
1525       // Try preferred rate first
1526       LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1527         err = Pa_OpenStream(&ctx->output_stream, NULL, &outputParams, preferred_rate, AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER, paClipOff,
1528                             callback, ctx);
1529       });
1530 
1531       if (err == paNoError) {
1532         actual_output_rate = preferred_rate;
1533         output_ok = true;
1534         log_info("✓ Output opened at preferred rate: %.0f Hz (matches input - optimal!)", preferred_rate);
1535       } else {
1536         log_warn("Failed to open output at %.0f Hz: %s, trying native rate %.0f Hz", preferred_rate,
1537                  Pa_GetErrorText(err), native_rate);
1538 
1539         // If first Pa_OpenStream call left a partial stream, clean it up before retrying
1540         if (ctx->output_stream) {
1541           log_debug("Closing partially-opened output stream from failed preferred rate");
1542           LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1543             Pa_CloseStream(ctx->output_stream);
1544           });
1545           ctx->output_stream = NULL;
1546         }
1547 
1548         // Fall back to native rate (still using blocking mode for output-only)
1549         LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1550           err = Pa_OpenStream(&ctx->output_stream, NULL, &outputParams, native_rate, AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER, paClipOff,
1551                               callback, ctx);
1552         });
1553 
1554         if (err == paNoError) {
1555           actual_output_rate = native_rate;
1556           output_ok = true;
1557           log_info("✓ Output opened at native rate: %.0f Hz (will need resampling)", native_rate);
1558         } else {
1559           log_warn("Failed to open output stream at native rate: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1560           // Clean up if fallback also failed
1561           if (ctx->output_stream) {
1562             log_debug("Closing partially-opened output stream from failed native rate");
1563             LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1564               Pa_CloseStream(ctx->output_stream);
1565             });
1566             ctx->output_stream = NULL;
1567           }
1568         }
1569       }
1570 
1571       // Store actual output rate for resampling
1572       if (output_ok) {
1573         ctx->output_device_rate = actual_output_rate;
1574         if (actual_output_rate != AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE) {
1575           log_warn("⚠️  Output rate mismatch: %.0f Hz output vs %.0f Hz input - resampling will be used",
1576                    actual_output_rate, (double)AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE);
1577         }
1578       }
1579     }
1580 
1581     // Open input stream only if we have input (skip for playback-only mode)
1582     bool input_ok = !has_input; // If no input, mark as OK (skip)
1583     if (has_input) {
1584       // Use pipeline sample rate (AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE)
1585       // In input-only mode, we don't need to match output device rate
1586       double input_stream_rate = AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE;
1587       LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1588         err = Pa_OpenStream(&ctx->input_stream, &inputParams, NULL, input_stream_rate, AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER, paClipOff,
1589                             input_callback, ctx);
1590       });
1591       input_ok = (err == paNoError);
1592 
1593       // If input failed, try device 0 as fallback (HDMI on BeaglePlay)
1594       if (!input_ok) {
1595         log_debug("Input failed - trying device 0 as fallback");
1596 
1597         // Clean up partial stream from first attempt before retrying
1598         if (ctx->input_stream) {
1599           log_debug("Closing partially-opened input stream from failed primary device");
1600           LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1601             Pa_CloseStream(ctx->input_stream);
1602           });
1603           ctx->input_stream = NULL;
1604         }
1605 
1606         PaStreamParameters fallback_input_params = inputParams;
1607         fallback_input_params.device = 0;
1608         const PaDeviceInfo *device_0_info = Pa_GetDeviceInfo(0);
1609         if (device_0_info && device_0_info->maxInputChannels > 0) {
1610           LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1611             err = Pa_OpenStream(&ctx->input_stream, &fallback_input_params, NULL, input_stream_rate,
1612                                 AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER, paClipOff, input_callback, ctx);
1613           });
1614           if (err == paNoError) {
1615             log_info("Input stream opened on device 0 (fallback from default)");
1616             input_ok = true;
1617           } else {
1618             log_warn("Fallback also failed on device 0: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1619             // Clean up if fallback also failed
1620             if (ctx->input_stream) {
1621               log_debug("Closing partially-opened input stream from failed fallback device");
1622               LOG_IO("portaudio", {

1624               });
1625               ctx->input_stream = NULL;
1626             }
1627           }
1628         }
1629       }
1630 
1631       if (!input_ok) {
1632         log_warn("Failed to open input stream: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1633       }
1634     }
1635 
1636     // Check if we got at least one stream working
1637     if (!input_ok && !output_ok) {
1638       // Neither stream works - fail completely
1639       audio_ring_buffer_destroy(ctx->render_buffer);
1640       ctx->render_buffer = NULL;
1641       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_AUDIO, "Failed to open both input and output streams");
1642     }
1643 
1644     // If output failed but input works, we can still send audio to server
1645     if (!output_ok && input_ok) {
1646       log_info("Output stream unavailable - continuing with input-only (can send audio to server)");
1647       ctx->output_stream = NULL;
1648     }
1649     // If input failed but output works, we can still receive audio from server
1650     if (!input_ok && output_ok) {
1651       log_info("Input stream unavailable - continuing with output-only (can receive audio from server)");
1652       ctx->input_stream = NULL;
1653     }
1654

1657       LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1658         err = Pa_StartStream(ctx->output_stream);
1659       });
1660       if (err != paNoError) {
1661         if (ctx->input_stream) {
1662           LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1663             Pa_CloseStream(ctx->input_stream);
1664           });
1665         }
1666         LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1667           Pa_CloseStream(ctx->output_stream);
1668         });
1669         ctx->input_stream = NULL;
1670         ctx->output_stream = NULL;
1671         audio_ring_buffer_destroy(ctx->render_buffer);
1672         ctx->render_buffer = NULL;
1673         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_AUDIO, "Failed to start output stream: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1674       }
1675     }
1676 
1677     // Start input stream if it's open
1678     if (ctx->input_stream) {
1679       LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1680         err = Pa_StartStream(ctx->input_stream);
1681       });
1682       if (err != paNoError) {
1683         if (ctx->output_stream) {
1684           LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1685             Pa_StopStream(ctx->output_stream);
1686           });
1687         }
1688         if (ctx->input_stream) {
1689           LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1690             Pa_CloseStream(ctx->input_stream);
1691           });
1692         }
1693         if (ctx->output_stream) {
1694           LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1695             Pa_CloseStream(ctx->output_stream);
1696           });
1697         }
1698         ctx->input_stream = NULL;
1699         ctx->output_stream = NULL;
1700         audio_ring_buffer_destroy(ctx->render_buffer);
1701         ctx->render_buffer = NULL;
1702         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_AUDIO, "Failed to start input stream: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1703       }
1704     }
1705 
1706     ctx->separate_streams = true;
1707     log_debug("Separate streams started successfully");
1708   } else {
1709     ctx->separate_streams = false;
1710     log_info("Full-duplex stream started (single callback, perfect AEC3 timing)");
1711   }
1712 
1713   audio_set_realtime_priority();
1714 
1715   // Start worker thread for heavy audio processing
1716   if (!ctx->worker_running) {
1717     atomic_store_bool(&ctx->worker_should_stop, false);
1718     if (asciichat_thread_create(&ctx->worker_thread, "audio_worker", audio_worker_thread, ctx) != 0) {
1719       // Failed to create worker thread - stop streams and cleanup

1728         LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1729           Pa_StopStream(ctx->input_stream);

1748 
1749   // Lock to update final state flags atomically
1750   mutex_lock(&ctx->state_mutex);
1751   ctx->running = true;

1753   mutex_unlock(&ctx->state_mutex);
1754   return ASCIICHAT_OK;

asciichat_error_t audio_stop_duplex
  (audio_context_t * ctx)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/audio/audio.h>


Stop full-duplex audio.


Parameters


ctx Audio context (must not be NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Stops the full-duplex audio stream and closes the stream.




Note


If not running, this function has no effect.



Buffers are not cleared by this function.




Definition at line 1757 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


1757                                                           {
1758   if (!ctx || !ctx->initialized) {
1759     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Audio context not initialized");
1760   }
1761 
1762   atomic_store_bool(&ctx->shutting_down, true);
1763 
1764   // Stop worker thread before stopping streams
1765   if (ctx->worker_running) {
1766     log_debug("Stopping worker thread");
1767     atomic_store_bool(&ctx->worker_should_stop, true);
1768     cond_signal(&ctx->worker_cond); // Wake up worker if waiting
1769     asciichat_thread_join(&ctx->worker_thread, NULL);
1770     ctx->worker_running = false;
1771     log_debug("Worker thread stopped successfully");
1772   }
1773

1775     audio_ring_buffer_clear(ctx->playback_buffer);
1776   }
1777 
1778   mutex_lock(&ctx->state_mutex);
1779 
1780   if (ctx->duplex_stream) {

1783     LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1784       err = Pa_StopStream(ctx->duplex_stream);
1785     });

1788     }
1789     log_debug("Closing duplex stream");

1795     } else {

1798     ctx->duplex_stream = NULL;
1799   }
1800 
1801   // Stop separate streams if used
1802   if (ctx->input_stream) {
1803     log_debug("Stopping input stream");
1804     PaError err;
1805     LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1806       err = Pa_StopStream(ctx->input_stream);
1807     });
1808     if (err != paNoError) {
1809       log_warn("Pa_StopStream input failed: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1810     }
1811     log_debug("Closing input stream");
1812     LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1813       err = Pa_CloseStream(ctx->input_stream);
1814     });
1815     if (err != paNoError) {
1816       log_warn("Pa_CloseStream input failed: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1817     } else {
1818       log_debug("Input stream closed successfully");
1819     }
1820     ctx->input_stream = NULL;
1821   }
1822 
1823   if (ctx->output_stream) {
1824     log_debug("Stopping output stream");
1825     PaError err;
1826     LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1827       err = Pa_StopStream(ctx->output_stream);
1828     });
1829     if (err != paNoError) {
1830       log_warn("Pa_StopStream output failed: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1831     }
1832     log_debug("Closing output stream");
1833     LOG_IO("portaudio", {
1834       err = Pa_CloseStream(ctx->output_stream);
1835     });
1836     if (err != paNoError) {
1837       log_warn("Pa_CloseStream output failed: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
1838     } else {
1839       log_debug("Output stream closed successfully");
1840     }
1841     ctx->output_stream = NULL;
1842   }
1843 
1844   // Cleanup render buffer
1845   if (ctx->render_buffer) {
1846     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(ctx->render_buffer);
1847     ctx->render_buffer = NULL;
1848   }
1849 
1850   ctx->running = false;
1851   ctx->separate_streams = false;
1852   mutex_unlock(&ctx->state_mutex);
1853 
1854   log_debug("Audio stopped");
1855   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1856 }

void
  audio_terminate_portaudio_final (void )


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/audio/audio.h>


Terminate PortAudio and free all device resources. This must be
    called at program shutdown to free device structures allocated by
    ALSA/libpulse during Pa_Initialize(). Should be called AFTER all audio
    contexts are destroyed and other session cleanup is complete.




Note


This is only needed if you want to ensure all memory is
  freed at exit. In normal operation, the OS will clean up when the process
  terminates.


This must be called to actually free device structures allocated
    by ALSA/libpulse. It should be called AFTER all audio contexts are destroyed
    and session cleanup is complete.

90 
91     PaError err;
92     LOG_IO("portaudio", {
93       err = Pa_Terminate();
94     });
95 
96     log_debug("[PORTAUDIO_TERM] Pa_Terminate() returned: %s", Pa_GetErrorText(err));
97   }
98 }

asciichat_error_t audio_validate_batch_params (const
  audio_batch_info_t * batch)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/audio/audio.h>


Validate audio batch parameters for sanity. Checks that parsed
    batch parameters are within acceptable ranges. Performs these checks:


    
  
  • batch_count > 0 and <= MAX_BATCH_SIZE
    • 
  
  • sample_rate is a standard rate (8000, 16000, 24000, 48000, etc.)
    • 
  
  • channels is 1 (mono) or 2 (stereo)
    • 
  
  • total_samples is consistent with batch_count
    • 



Parameters


batch Pointer to audio_batch_info_t struct
  to validate


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK if batch parameters are valid



ERROR_INVALID_PARAM if any parameter is out of range




Usage:




audio_batch_info_t batch;
audio_parse_batch_header(data, len, &batch);
if (audio_validate_batch_params(&batch) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {


    log_error("Audio batch parameters invalid");


    return;
}



Definition at line 2033 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


2033                                                                                {
2034   if (!batch) {
2035     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch info pointer is NULL");
2036   }
2037 
2038   // Validate batch_count
2039   if (batch->batch_count == 0) {
2040     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch count cannot be zero");
2041   }
2042 
2043   // Check for reasonable max (256 frames per batch is very generous)
2044   if (batch->batch_count > 256) {
2045     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch count too large (batch_count=%u, max=256)", batch->batch_count);
2046   }
2047 
2048   // Validate channels (1=mono, 2=stereo, max 8 for multi-channel)
2049   if (batch->channels == 0 || batch->channels > 8) {
2050     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid channel count (channels=%u, valid=1-8)", batch->channels);
2051   }
2052 
2053   // Validate sample rate
2054   if (!audio_is_supported_sample_rate(batch->sample_rate)) {
2055     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Unsupported sample rate (sample_rate=%u)", batch->sample_rate);
2056   }
2057 
2058   // Check for reasonable sample counts
2059   if (batch->total_samples == 0) {
2060     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch has zero samples");
2061   }
2062 
2063   // Each batch typically has samples_per_frame worth of samples
2064   // For 48kHz at 20ms per frame: 48000 * 0.02 = 960 samples per frame
2065   // With max 256 frames, that's up to ~245k samples per batch
2066   if (batch->total_samples > 1000000) {
2067     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch sample count suspiciously large (total_samples=%u)",
2068                      batch->total_samples);
2069   }
2070 
2071   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2072 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    audio_is_supported_sample_rate(),
    audio_batch_info_t::batch_count, audio_batch_info_t::channels,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, audio_batch_info_t::sample_rate,
    SET_ERRNO, and audio_batch_info_t::total_samples.






asciichat_error_t audio_write_samples
  (audio_context_t * ctx, const float * buffer, int num_samples)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/audio/audio.h>


Write audio samples to playback buffer.


Parameters


ctx Audio context (must not be NULL)


buffer Input buffer of audio samples (must not be NULL)


num_samples Number of samples to write (must be > 0)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Writes audio samples to the playback ring buffer for playback.
    Samples should be in floating point format (-1.0 to 1.0 range). This
    function is non-blocking and writes as many samples as buffer space
  allows.




Note


Actual number of samples written may be less than
  num_samples if buffer doesn't have enough space.



Samples are queued for playback by the playback thread.




Thread-safe: Can be called from any thread.




Warning


Buffer must contain at least num_samples float
  values.


Definition at line 1869 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


1869                                                                                                   {
1870   if (!ctx || !ctx->initialized || !buffer || num_samples <= 0) {
1871     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: ctx=%p, buffer=%p, num_samples=%d", ctx, buffer,
1872                      num_samples);
1873   }
1874 
1875   // Don't accept new audio data during shutdown - this prevents garbage/beeps
1876   if (atomic_load_bool(&ctx->shutting_down)) {
1877     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Silently discard
1878   }
1879 
1880   asciichat_error_t result = audio_ring_buffer_write(ctx->playback_buffer, buffer, num_samples);
1881 
1882   return result;
1883 }

Find peak absolute value in buffer.

Scans buffer for the sample with highest absolute value. Useful

float
  compressor_process_sample (compressor_t * comp, float
  sidechain)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/audio/mixer.h>


Process a single sample through compressor.


Parameters


comp Compressor structure


sidechain Input level for gain reduction calculation


Returns


Compressed output level


Processes sidechain input through compressor to calculate gain
    reduction. Returns compressed output level (not actual audio sample - use
    for sidechain).




Note


This calculates gain reduction, not actual audio
  compression. Apply the gain to audio samples separately.


Definition at line 88 of file mixer.c.


88                                                                      {
89   float x = fabsf(sidechain);
90 
91   // Update envelope with attack/release
92   if (x > comp->envelope)
93     comp->envelope = comp->attack_coeff * comp->envelope + (1.0f - comp->attack_coeff) * x;
94   else
95     comp->envelope = comp->release_coeff * comp->envelope + (1.0f - comp->release_coeff) * x;
96 
97   // Calculate gain reduction
98   float level_dB = linear_to_db(comp->envelope);
99   float gr_dB = compressor_gain_reduction_db(comp, level_dB);
100   float target_lin = db_to_linear(gr_dB + comp->makeup_dB);
101 
102   // Smooth gain changes
103   if (target_lin < comp->gain_lin)
104     comp->gain_lin = comp->attack_coeff * comp->gain_lin + (1.0f - comp->attack_coeff) * target_lin;
105   else
106     comp->gain_lin = comp->release_coeff * comp->gain_lin + (1.0f - comp->release_coeff) * target_lin;
107 
108   return comp->gain_lin;
109 }

Set compressor parameters.


Parameters

66   comp->attack_coeff = expf(-1.0f / (attack_tau * comp->sample_rate + 1e-12f));

116 
117   // Set default parameters
118   // threshold_dB: sources below this aren't considered "speaking"
119   // leader_margin_dB: sources within this margin of loudest are all "leaders"
120   // atten_dB: how much to attenuate non-leaders (was -12dB, too aggressive)
121   duck->threshold_dB = -45.0f;            // More lenient threshold
122   duck->leader_margin_dB = 6.0f;          // Wider margin = more sources treated as leaders
123   duck->atten_dB = -6.0f;                 // Only -6dB attenuation (was -12dB)
124   duck->attack_ns = 10 * NS_PER_MS_INT;   // Slower attack (10ms)
125   duck->release_ns = 200 * NS_PER_MS_INT; // Slower release (200ms)

127   duck->gain = NULL;

138     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER_OVERFLOW, "Ducking envelope array size overflow: %d sources", num_sources);
139   }
140   duck->envelope = SAFE_MALLOC(envelope_size, float *);

142     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate ducking envelope array");

147     SAFE_FREE(duck->envelope);
148     duck->envelope = NULL;
149     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER_OVERFLOW, "Ducking gain array size overflow: %d sources", num_sources);

void
  ducking_process_frame (ducking_t * duck, float * envelopes, float *
  gains, int num_sources)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/audio/mixer.h>


Process a frame of audio through ducking system.


Parameters


duck Ducking structure


envelopes Array of per-source envelope levels (input)


gains Array of per-source ducking gains (output)


num_sources Number of sources to process


Calculates ducking gains for each source based on envelope levels.
    Outputs per-source gain multipliers to apply to audio samples.




Note


Gains are calculated from envelope levels, identifying
  leaders and applying attenuation to non-leaders.


Definition at line 185 of file mixer.c.


185                                                                                              {
186   // Find the loudest source
187   float max_dB = -120.0f;
188   float env_dB[MIXER_MAX_SOURCES];
189 
190   for (int i = 0; i < num_sources; i++) {
191     env_dB[i] = linear_to_db(envelopes[i]);
192     if (env_dB[i] > max_dB)
193       max_dB = env_dB[i];
194   }
195 
196   // Calculate ducking gain for each source
197   float leader_cut = db_to_linear(duck->atten_dB);
198 
199   for (int i = 0; i < num_sources; i++) {
200     bool is_speaking = env_dB[i] > duck->threshold_dB;
201     bool is_leader = is_speaking && (env_dB[i] >= max_dB - duck->leader_margin_dB);
202 
203     float target;
204     if (is_speaking && !is_leader) {
205       target = leader_cut;
206     } else {
207       target = 1.0f;
208     }
209 
210     // Smooth gain transitions
211     if (target < gains[i])
212       gains[i] = duck->attack_coeff * gains[i] + (1.0f - duck->attack_coeff) * target;
213     else
214       gains[i] = duck->release_coeff * gains[i] + (1.0f - duck->release_coeff) * target;
215   }
216 }






References ducking_t::attack_coeff,
    ducking_t::atten_dB, db_to_linear(),
    ducking_t::leader_margin_dB, linear_to_db(),
    MIXER_MAX_SOURCES, ducking_t::release_coeff, and
    ducking_t::threshold_dB.

#include
  <include/ascii-chat/audio/mixer.h>


Apply linear fade to buffer in-place.


Parameters


buffer Audio buffer (modified in-place)


count Number of samples


start_gain Gain at start of buffer


end_gain Gain at end of buffer


Applies linear interpolation from start_gain to end_gain across
    buffer. Use start_gain=0, end_gain=1 for fade-in; start_gain=1, end_gain=0
    for fade-out.

1109   if (!buffer || count <= 0)
1110     return;
1111 
1112   float step = (end_gain - start_gain) / (float)count;
1113   float gain = start_gain;
1114   for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
1115     buffer[i] *= gain;
1116     gain += step;
1117   }
1118 }

979 
980   // Calculate filter coefficient using RC time constant formula
981   // alpha = dt / (RC + dt) where RC = 1 / (2 * pi * fc)
982   float dt = 1.0f / sample_rate;
983   float rc = 1.0f / (2.0f * (float)M_PI * cutoff_hz);
984   filter->alpha = dt / (rc + dt);
985 
986   lowpass_filter_reset(filter);
987 }

  mixer_add_source (mixer_t * mixer, const char * client_id,

#include
  <include/ascii-chat/audio/mixer.h>


Add an audio source to the mixer.


Parameters


mixer Audio mixer


client_id Client ID for this source


buffer Pointer to client's audio ring buffer


Returns


Mixer source index on success, -1 on failure (max sources
  reached)


Adds a new audio source to the mixer. Client ID is mapped to a
    mixer source index for efficient lookup during processing.




Note

378                                                                                          {
379   if (!mixer || !buffer)
380     return -1;
381 
382   // OPTIMIZATION 2: Acquire write lock for source modification
383   rwlock_wrlock(&mixer->source_lock);
384 
385   // Find an empty slot
386   int slot = -1;
387   for (int i = 0; i < mixer->max_sources; i++) {
388     if (mixer->source_ids[i] == NULL) {
389       slot = i;
390       break;
391     }
392   }
393 
394   if (slot == -1) {
395     rwlock_wrunlock(&mixer->source_lock);
396     log_warn("Mixer: No available slots for client %s", client_id);
397     return -1;
398   }
399 
400   mixer->source_buffers[slot] = buffer;
401   // Duplicate the client_id string - we own this memory now
402   mixer->source_ids[slot] = SAFE_MALLOC(strlen(client_id) + 1, char *);
403   if (!mixer->source_ids[slot]) {
404     log_warn("Mixer: Failed to allocate memory for client_id %s", client_id);
405     mixer->source_buffers[slot] = NULL;
406     rwlock_wrunlock(&mixer->source_lock);
407     return -1;
408   }
409   strcpy((char *)mixer->source_ids[slot], client_id);
410   mixer->source_active[slot] = true;
411   mixer->num_sources++;
412 
413   // OPTIMIZATION 1: Update bitset optimization structures
414   mixer->active_sources_mask |= (1ULL << slot); // Set bit for this slot
415   // Hash table optimization disabled for string-based client_ids (linear search is acceptable for small mixer sizes)
416 
417   rwlock_wrunlock(&mixer->source_lock);
418 
419   log_info("Mixer: Added source for client %s at slot %d", client_id, slot);
420   return slot;
421 }

220   // Validate parameters

223     return NULL;
224   }
225 
226   if (sample_rate <= 0 || sample_rate > 192000) {
227     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid sample_rate: %d (must be 1-192000)", sample_rate);
228     return NULL;
229   }
230 
231   mixer_t *mixer;
232   mixer = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(mixer_t), mixer_t *);
233   if (!mixer) {
234     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate mixer structure");
235     return NULL;
236   }
237 
238   mixer->num_sources = 0;
239   mixer->max_sources = max_sources;
240   mixer->sample_rate = sample_rate;
241 
242   // Allocate source management arrays with overflow checking
243   size_t buffers_size = 0;
244   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)max_sources, sizeof(audio_ring_buffer_t *), &buffers_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
245     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER_OVERFLOW, "Mixer source buffers array overflow: %d sources", max_sources);
246     SAFE_FREE(mixer);
247     return NULL;
248   }
249   mixer->source_buffers = SAFE_MALLOC(buffers_size, audio_ring_buffer_t **);
250   if (!mixer->source_buffers) {
251     SAFE_FREE(mixer);
252     return NULL;
253   }
254 
255   size_t ids_size = 0;
256   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)max_sources, sizeof(const char *), &ids_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
257     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER_OVERFLOW, "Mixer source IDs array overflow: %d sources", max_sources);
258     SAFE_FREE(mixer->source_buffers);
259     SAFE_FREE(mixer);
260     return NULL;
261   }
262   mixer->source_ids = SAFE_MALLOC(ids_size, const char **);
263   if (!mixer->source_ids) {
264     SAFE_FREE(mixer->source_buffers);
265     SAFE_FREE(mixer);
266     return NULL;
267   }
268 
269   size_t active_size = 0;
270   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)max_sources, sizeof(bool), &active_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
271     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER_OVERFLOW, "Mixer source active array overflow: %d sources", max_sources);
272     SAFE_FREE(mixer->source_buffers);
273     SAFE_FREE(mixer->source_ids);
274     SAFE_FREE(mixer);
275     return NULL;
276   }
277   mixer->source_active = SAFE_MALLOC(active_size, bool *);
278   if (!mixer->source_active) {

280     SAFE_FREE(mixer->source_ids);
281     SAFE_FREE(mixer);
282     return NULL;
283   }
284 
285   // Initialize arrays
286   SAFE_MEMSET((void *)mixer->source_buffers, buffers_size, 0, buffers_size);
287   SAFE_MEMSET(mixer->source_ids, ids_size, 0, ids_size);
288   SAFE_MEMSET(mixer->source_active, active_size, 0, active_size);
289 
290   // OPTIMIZATION 1: Initialize bitset optimization structures
291   mixer->active_sources_mask = 0ULL; // No sources active initially
292   for (int i = 0; i < 256; i++) {
293     mixer->source_id_to_index[i] = MIXER_HASH_INVALID;
294   }
295 
296   // Allocate mix buffer BEFORE rwlock_init so cleanup path is correct
297   size_t mix_buffer_size = 0;
298   if (checked_size_mul(MIXER_FRAME_SIZE, sizeof(float), &mix_buffer_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
299     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER_OVERFLOW, "Mixer mix buffer size overflow: %zu samples", (size_t)MIXER_FRAME_SIZE);
300     SAFE_FREE(mixer->source_buffers);
301     SAFE_FREE(mixer->source_ids);
302     SAFE_FREE(mixer->source_active);
303     SAFE_FREE(mixer);
304     return NULL;
305   }
306   mixer->mix_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(mix_buffer_size, float *);
307   if (!mixer->mix_buffer) {
308     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate mix buffer");
309     SAFE_FREE(mixer->source_buffers);
310     SAFE_FREE(mixer->source_ids);
311     SAFE_FREE(mixer->source_active);
312     SAFE_FREE(mixer);
313     return NULL;
314   }
315 
316   // OPTIMIZATION 2: Initialize reader-writer lock
317   if (rwlock_init(&mixer->source_lock, "audio_mixer_sources") != 0) {
318     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to initialize mixer source lock");
319     SAFE_FREE(mixer->source_buffers);
320     SAFE_FREE(mixer->source_ids);
321     SAFE_FREE(mixer->source_active);
322     SAFE_FREE(mixer->mix_buffer);
323     SAFE_FREE(mixer);
324     return NULL;
325   }
326 
327   // Set crowd scaling parameters
328   mixer->crowd_alpha = 0.5f; // Square root scaling
329   mixer->base_gain = 1.0f;   // Unity gain - let compressor handle loudness, avoid clipping
330 
331   // Initialize processing
332   if (ducking_init(&mixer->ducking, max_sources, (float)sample_rate) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
333     rwlock_destroy(&mixer->source_lock);
334     SAFE_FREE(mixer->source_buffers);
335     SAFE_FREE(mixer->source_ids);
336     SAFE_FREE(mixer->source_active);
337     SAFE_FREE(mixer->mix_buffer);
338     SAFE_FREE(mixer);
339     return NULL;
340   }
341   compressor_init(&mixer->compressor, (float)sample_rate);
342 
343   log_debug("Audio mixer created: max_sources=%d, sample_rate=%d", max_sources, sample_rate);
344 
345   // Register mixer and its sync primitives for debugging
346   NAMED_REGISTER_MIXER(mixer, "audio_mixer", NULL);
347   NAMED_REGISTER_RWLOCK(&mixer->source_lock, "source_lock", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(mixer));
348 
349   return mixer;
350 }

371   SAFE_FREE(mixer->source_ids);

374   SAFE_FREE(mixer);
